ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Continued To May-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Darren Cordano for executor Michael C. Brumbaugh (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Close of Estate.

Mr. Cordano reported that a settlement related to the estate had been finalized and that the estate was ready to be closed. He stated that the petition to close the estate could be filed within approximately one month.

The matter was continued to May 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Darren Cordano, for Michael Brumbaugh, Executor.

At the court's query, counsel reports on the status of the settlement to the Estate and of the related civil action. Counsel confirms that once the Estate property is sold, the funds from that sale will be used to satisfy the terms of the civil action, the intent is to seek termination of the subject matter.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Mar-02-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Robles. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-04-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eurik O'Bryant, via Zoom, for Michael Brumbaugh, via Zoom, Petitioner; Marissa Saludis, via Zoom, Interested Party; James Viscarra, in court, Interested Party; Alexis Gaines, via Zoom, Beneficiary.

Oral testimony regarding the decedent's will is provided by Marissa Saludis, including an objection, James Viscarra, and Alexis Gaines. The court advises the parties as to what would happen should the court grant the petition and appoint Mr. Brumbaugh as Executor specifically, that it would not affect the beneficiaries or heirs. Mr. O'Bryant summarizes the previous calling of this matter and points to Ms. Saludis appearing this morning without counsel and that formal challenges have not been formally filed up to now. Additional discussion is made regarding the positions of the Interested Parties and the Beneficiary.

The court overrules Ms. Saludis' oral objections and grants the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The lost will dated Aug-05-2016 and Codicil dated Sep-18-2017 are admitted to probate. Michael C. Brumbaugh is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Apr-24-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Continued To Apr-04-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Simron Sekhon, via CourtCall, for Michael Brumbaugh, Petitioner; Marissa Salides and Melvin Salides, in court, in propria persona Interested Parties; Michael Mercado, appearing in court with Marissa and Melvin Salides; Alexis Gaines, in court, Beneficiary.

The parties discuss the scope of today's proceedings stating that his client is appointed Executor in the decedent's will and asks the court to confirm with the parties present in court if they are appearing to object. Marissa Salides and Melvin Salides ("The Salides') offer oral testimony regarding their appearance this morning and report that they have the original will. The court confirms the Salides' relationship to the decedent and confirms that they do not have an attorney but have consulted with a local probate attorney. The court and the parties discuss their individual positions and confirms with the Salides' that they may possibly file an objection and suggests that they consider hiring an attorney to assist them with the process. Mr. Sekhon requests that the interested parties to provide their addresses for notice purposes. The Interested Parties provide Mr. Sekhon with their addresses.

The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to Apr-04-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Salides' are advised that if they are going to hire an attorney, they must do so by Mar-21-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.
